$comment: Doctor checks registry — partial. Some checks need real Python logic
(parsing /proc, regex over sshd_config drop-ins). Those stay as code
in doctor.py. The simpler 'is package X installed?' style checks live
here as data, so adding a new advisory is a JSON edit.
Each check has cross-distro fix commands keyed by package-manager
target          package name / unit name / binary name
fix             { apt: '...', dnf: '...', zypper: '...', pacman: '...', apk: '...' }

checks.name: auditd
checks.why: auditd records security-relevant kernel events (syscalls, file access). Useful for forensics after an incident.

fix.apt: sudo apt-get install -y auditd && sudo systemctl enable --now auditd
fix.dnf: sudo dnf install -y audit && sudo systemctl enable --now auditd
fix.zypper: sudo zypper install -y audit && sudo systemctl enable --now auditd
fix.pacman: sudo pacman -S --noconfirm audit && sudo systemctl enable --now auditd
fix.apk: sudo apk add audit && sudo rc-update add auditd

checks.name: rkhunter (rootkit scanner)
checks.why: Periodic rootkit scanning catches LD_PRELOAD-style and kernel-module rootkits.

fix.apt: sudo apt-get install -y rkhunter && sudo rkhunter --update && sudo rkhunter --check
fix.dnf: sudo dnf install -y rkhunter && sudo rkhunter --update && sudo rkhunter --check
fix.zypper: sudo zypper install -y rkhunter && sudo rkhunter --update && sudo rkhunter --check

checks.name: lynis (system-hardening audit)
checks.why: Lynis runs a deeper hardening audit than aiterm doctor — recommended for periodic review.

fix.apt: sudo apt-get install -y lynis && sudo lynis audit system
fix.dnf: sudo dnf install -y lynis && sudo lynis audit system
fix.zypper: sudo zypper install -y lynis && sudo lynis audit system
fix.pacman: sudo pacman -S --noconfirm lynis && sudo lynis audit system

checks.name: needrestart (post-update reboot detection)
checks.why: Detects when services use deleted libraries (after a security update) and need restarting.

fix.apt: sudo apt-get install -y needrestart && sudo needrestart

checks.name: system logging
checks.why: Without persistent system logs, post-incident forensics is impossible.

fix.apt: # journald should be active by default — investigate why it's down
fix.dnf: # journald should be active by default — investigate why it's down
